---
title: "Teaching"
toc: false
---

## Instructor

::: {.teaching-entry}
#### Global Development Issues
[Erasmus University Rotterdam · 2025--2026]{.teaching-meta}
Quantitative research design for policy evaluation, including causal inference and panel data estimation, with hands-on R programming labs using real policy datasets.
:::

::: {.teaching-entry}
#### Bachelor Project: Digital Governance, Platform Labour, and Power in the Digital Economy
[Erasmus University Rotterdam · 2025--2026]{.teaching-meta}
Supervising the MISOC Bachelor Project (Group 16). [Course materials](bachelor-project/) (password protected).
:::

::: {.teaching-entry}
#### EPPS Math & Coding Camp
[The University of Texas at Dallas · August 2024]{.teaching-meta}
Five-day intensive for incoming Ph.D. students: R programming, data manipulation, and visualization. Course website: [eppsmathandcodingcamp.com](https://eppsmathandcodingcamp.com/).
:::

## Teaching Assistant

::: {.teaching-entry}
[The University of Texas at Dallas · 2020--2023]{.teaching-meta}
Research Design (EPPS 2301) · Class, Status, and Power (SOC 4302) · Political Economy of Industrialized Countries (IPEC 4301) · Government and Politics of Afghanistan (PSCI 4396) · Applied Regression (EPPS 6316) · Political Violence and Conflict in Cyberspace (PPPE/PSCI 6302) · Introduction to Quantitative Methods (EPPS 6313) · Foreign Aid and Development (IPEC/PSCI 4318)
:::
